title: Fixed log prob result generation

step_plan.summary: Add a merged OpenAI-style "logprobs" field to response choice types and implement merging logic in the JSONFFI layer to parse per-token logprob fragments and produce a single tokens/token_logprobs/top_logprobs object per choice so it is serialized in streaming and final responses.

steps.operation: Add storage for merged logprobs to OpenAI response choice types
steps.reason: We need a place on the response choice objects (both streaming and final) to hold the merged OpenAI-style "logprobs" object so it can be serialized. Adding an optional picojson::value (or picojson::object wrapper) member to ChatCompletionStreamResponseChoice and ChatCompletionResponseChoice enables storing the merged result produced later in the pipeline.
steps.side_effects: Changes struct/class layout in header — requires full rebuild. Downstream code that constructs these choices may need to be updated to default-initialize or clear the new member. No API contract break for callers that ignore unknown fields, but serialized JSON may include new keys once populated; update tests and any code that parses exactly-shaped JSON.

steps.operation: Serialize logprobs in choice AsJSON methods
steps.reason: ChatCompletionStreamResponseChoice::AsJSON and ChatCompletionResponseChoice::AsJSON must include the new "logprobs" field in the output JSON when the merged member is set. Without this change the merged logprobs stored on the choice will never appear in the serialized OpenAI-compatible JSON.
steps.side_effects: Alters output JSON shape when logprobs are present — consumers should accept the new field. Tests that assert exact JSON strings must be updated. If the logprobs member contains malformed picojson::value, ensure serialization handles it gracefully (the implementation should check presence/validity before inserting).

steps.operation: Implement merging/parsing of per-token logprob JSON fragments and attach merged object to choices
steps.reason: JSONFFIEngineImpl::GetResponseFromStreamOutput receives RequestStreamOutput which carries per-token logprob JSON fragments in group_delta_logprob_json_strs. This function must parse each fragment (picojson::parse), extract token text and logprob, transform per-token top_logprobs into an array of mapping objects {token: logprob}, build the OpenAI-style merged object {"text_offset": null, "token_logprobs": [...], "tokens": [...], "top_logprobs": [...]} and set it on the corresponding ChatCompletionStreamResponseChoice (and/or ChatCompletionResponseChoice) member so serialization emits it.
steps.side_effects: Introduces CPU and memory overhead due to JSON parsing and object construction during streaming; ensure efficient parsing and avoid throwing errors to callers. Must handle parse failures robustly by skipping invalid fragments and not crashing the stream. Affects streaming and final responses: tests and any downstream components consuming logprobs need to be updated/verified. May require synchronization if multiple threads touch the same RequestStreamOutput structure (verify existing concurrency guarantees).
